title: Ipomoea pudibunda
intro: Ipomoea pudibunda, also known as the "prickly morning glory," is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual disorders, due to its sedative and estrogenic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea pudibunda include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ipomoea pudibunda is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its calming and balancing effects on the body.
taxonomy: Ipomoea pudibunda is classified within the plant family Convolvulaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Ipomoea, which comprises over 600 species of flowering plants. The species is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Within the Convolvulaceae family, Ipomoea pudibunda is often placed in the subfamily Convolvuloideae. The classification of Ipomoea pudibunda is subject to ongoing research and potential revisions in the field of botany.
